summary:
Claim: Cardiac dysrhythmia during dental extraction arises indirectly from pain-triggered autonomic reflexes, not direct neural control of the heart.  
Evidence: The trigeminal nerve transmits nociceptive signals from teeth/jaw; intense pain during extraction activates central reflexes leading to sympathetic overactivity and transient tachycardia or arrhythmias. None of the other cranial nerves (facial, trochlear, oculomotor) have established links to autonomic regulation or cardiac rhythm modulation.  
Answer: C (Trigeminal nerve)

None of the other cranial nerves (facial, trochlear, oculomotor) have established links to autonomic regulation or cardiac rhythm modulation.  
Answer: C (Trigeminal nerve). Answer choice: C
